title: Gently Grilled Japanese beef Gyoza
description: Inspired by the Shikoku region of Japan, this dish is a unique twist on traditional Japanese beef Gyoza, featuring lean minced beef and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine minced beef, spring onions, ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil, cumin, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-low heat.
Lay a Gyoza wrapper on a flat surface and place a small spoonful of the beef mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wrapper.
Fold the wrapper into a half-moon shape, pressing the edges together to seal the Gyoza.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Place the Gyoza on the grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on the first side, or until the bottom is lightly browned.
Flip the Gyoza over and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the other side is also lightly browned.
Serve the Gyoza hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
